subject: ap-calculus-bc
theme: The derivative captures instantaneous rate of change — defined as a limit, computed with rules, and used to find tangent lines and analyze motion.

core_concepts.title: Derivative as a Limit
core_concepts.detail: The derivative $f'(x) = \lim_{h \to 0} \frac{f(x+h) - f(x)}{h}$ is the instantaneous rate of change of $f$ at $x$. It equals the slope of the tangent line to the graph at that point.

core_concepts.title: Power Rule
core_concepts.detail: $\frac{d}{dx}[x^n] = nx^{n-1}$ for any real $n$. Combined with the constant multiple and sum rules, this handles all polynomial derivatives.

core_concepts.title: Product and Quotient Rules
core_concepts.detail: Product: $\frac{d}{dx}[fg] = f'g + fg'$. Quotient: $\frac{d}{dx}\left[\frac{f}{g}\right] = \frac{f'g - fg'}{g^2}$. Apply when two functions are multiplied or divided.

core_concepts.title: Trigonometric Derivatives
core_concepts.detail: $\frac{d}{dx}[\sin x] = \cos x$, $\frac{d}{dx}[\cos x] = -\sin x$, $\frac{d}{dx}[\tan x] = \sec^2 x$, $\frac{d}{dx}[\sec x] = \sec x \tan x$, $\frac{d}{dx}[\csc x] = -\csc x \cot x$, $\frac{d}{dx}[\cot x] = -\csc^2 x$.

core_concepts.title: Exponential and Logarithmic Derivatives
core_concepts.detail: $\frac{d}{dx}[e^x] = e^x$ and $\frac{d}{dx}[\ln x] = \frac{1}{x}$. More generally, $\frac{d}{dx}[a^x] = a^x \ln a$ and $\frac{d}{dx}[\log_a x] = \frac{1}{x \ln a}$.

core_concepts.title: Differentiability and Continuity
core_concepts.detail: Differentiability implies continuity, but not vice versa. A function can be continuous but not differentiable at corners, cusps, or vertical tangents.

core_concepts.title: Tangent Line Equation
core_concepts.detail: The tangent line at $x = a$ is $y - f(a) = f'(a)(x - a)$. This is a linearization of $f$ near $a$ and provides a first-order approximation.

formulas.name: Limit Definition of Derivative
formulas.description: Defines the derivative as the limit of the difference quotient. This is how derivatives are rigorously defined before shortcut rules.

formulas.name: Power Rule
formulas.description: Bring the exponent down as a coefficient and reduce the exponent by 1. Works for any real number n, including negatives and fractions.

formulas.name: Product Rule
formulas.description: Derivative of the first times the second, plus the first times derivative of the second. Use when two functions are multiplied.

formulas.name: Quotient Rule
formulas.description: Low d-high minus high d-low, all over low squared. Use when one function is divided by another.

formulas.name: Tangent Line
formulas.description: Point-slope form of the tangent line at x = a. You need the point (a, f(a)) and the slope f'(a).
formulas.example: For $f(x) = x^3$ at $x = 2$: $f(2) = 8$, $f'(2) = 12$, so tangent line is $y - 8 = 12(x - 2)$.

exam_tip: Memorize all six trig derivative pairs — they appear frequently on both MCQ and FRQ sections. Differentiability implies continuity, but continuity does NOT imply differentiability.
